# CrossTenantIsolation integration spec: alice add_memory returns isError post-substrate-fix

## Context

Surfaced 2026-05-07 by Lane C [#10899](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10899) integration row CI on rebased head `56ed56e92` after the full substrate-fix cascade (#10904 + #10914 + #10916) merged. The basic healthcheck and AuthRejection specs PASS (proving the substrate is sound), but `CrossTenantIsolation.integration.spec.mjs:43` fails when alice's `add_memory` MCP tool call returns `result.isError === true` (server-side application error, not transport).

Per agreement with @tobiu, deferring this with a `NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI` skip-guard to unblock Lane C, with this dedicated ticket for separate investigation.

## The Problem

`test/playwright/integration/CrossTenantIsolation.integration.spec.mjs:20:5` › *"alice and bob only retrieve their own tenant-tagged memories"*

**Symptom:** alice's first `add_memory` call returns `{isError: true}` from MC's tool dispatch. The error surfaces at line 43 in `callJsonTool` via the `expect(result.isError).not.toBe(true)` assertion in `mcpClient.mjs:78`.

**What we know:**
- Empirical evidence: [Lane C run 25511367136](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/actions/runs/25511367136) integration job, post-substrate-fix.
- AuthRejection test PASSES — so the trust-proxy-identity gate is firing correctly (alice's identity-bearing client gets through; identity-less client gets 401).
- Basic healthcheck (Lane A) PASSES — so the per-session McpServer flow is sound.
- Sustained-liveness composability (5s/1s window) PASSES — so `assertSustainedHealth` works.
- This SPECIFIC failure is application-layer: alice CONNECTS successfully + sends `add_memory` request + MC returns tool-level error.

**What we don't know:**
- The actual error message in `result.content` (CI log doesn't surface it; needs `docker compose logs mc-server` capture or in-test logging of the error response).
- Whether the issue is in `add_memory` schema validation, ChromaDB write under tenant-scoped userId, or some other application-layer cause.

## The Architectural Reality

- [`test/playwright/integration/CrossTenantIsolation.integration.spec.mjs`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/test/playwright/integration/CrossTenantIsolation.integration.spec.mjs) — Lane A's spec from [#10901](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10901), tests the `userId`-scoped tenant isolation on MC.
- The `add_memory` tool path: writes Chroma collection metadata-tagged with the auth-resolved `userId`. With trust-proxy-identity active and alice's `X-PREFERRED-USERNAME: alice` header, the `userId` should resolve to `alice`.
- Possible failure surfaces: schema validation, Chroma collection-create-on-first-write, embedding service, the per-session McpServer's request-context propagation.

## The Fix (Investigation Required)

This is an **investigation-shaped ticket** — root cause not yet diagnosed.

**Phase 1**: capture the actual `result.content[0].text` error message via either:
- (a) Add `docker compose logs mc-server` step to Lane C workflow on integration failure (workflow-side instrumentation).
- (b) Modify `mcpClient.mjs:callJsonTool` to log the error result before assertion (test-side instrumentation).
- (c) Reproduce locally with Docker available + read MC log directly.

**Phase 2**: based on Phase 1 evidence, determine fix shape (could be application bug in MC's add_memory, test-spec setup gap, schema validation, or something else).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Phase 1 diagnostic completes: actual error message from MC's `add_memory` response surfaced.
- [ ] Phase 2 fix lands based on Phase 1 evidence (likely 1 commit + reverting the `NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI` skip-guard from the spec).
- [ ] CrossTenantIsolation integration spec passes in CI (`npm run test-integration` with all servers up).

## Out of Scope

- HeartbeatPropagation uptime-equality bug (filed as separate sibling ticket).
- Refactoring the test to use a different tenant-isolation testing pattern.

## Related

- **Surfacing context:** [Lane C CI run 25511367136](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/actions/runs/25511367136) integration job — first end-to-end run with all 5 substrate fixes applied.
- **Predecessor substrate work:** [#10915](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10915) → [#10916](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10916) (per-session McpServer + NEO_AUTH_* binding); [#10913](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10913) → [#10914](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10914) (Chroma bash TCP probe); etc.
- **Originating spec:** [#10895](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10895) → [#10901](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10901) Lane A (GPT authored the cross-tenant isolation spec).
- **Skip-guard PR:** TBD (filed concurrently with this ticket).

- 2026-05-07T17:42:31Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `094c3e7` - "fix(ci): activate NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI for bucket-skip pattern (#10897)

Per @neo-gpt's empirical RA on PR #10919 (skip-guards for bucket F
application-spec deferrals): the test-side guards are inert without
the env var actually being set in the target CI path.

This adds NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI=true to the Run-tests step's env block —
canonical activation for the bucket-skip pattern that all the merged
unit-suite skips (#10907 bucket A+E, Gemini's bucket B+D, GPT's
#10910 bucket C) AND the in-flight integration-suite skips
(#10919 bucket F = #10917 + #10918) depend on.

Single workflow knob; symmetric across both unit + integration matrix
rows when unit gets re-added in the planned followup PR.

Closes the gate-gap GPT correctly named: skip activation contract was
incomplete on this PR's prior iteration."
- 2026-05-07T17:58:33Z @tobiu referenced in commit `ad04351` - "test(integration): defer 2 application-spec failures pending investigation (#10917) (#10919)

Adds NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI guards to two integration specs that fail at the
application layer post-substrate-fix-cascade. Substrate is sound:
healthcheck.spec.mjs and AuthRejection.spec.mjs both pass on the same
run, proving per-session McpServer + NEO_AUTH_* binding are correct.

The 2 deferred specs are:
- CrossTenantIsolation: alice add_memory returns isError (#10917) —
  application-layer error in MC's tool dispatch; root cause unknown
  pending diagnostic capture of MC's error response.
- HeartbeatPropagation: 30s-window monotonic uptime assertion fails
  because two consecutive samples report identical process.uptime()
  values (#10918) — possibly per-session McpServer regression from
  #10916 OR test-spec strictness; diagnostic Phase 1 needed.

Bucket F pattern: 'CI-skip: bucket F application-spec deferral — see
#NNNNN'. Skip-message string carries the ticket reference for future
cleanup grep when Phase 1 diagnostic + Phase 2 fix land.

This unblocks Lane C #10899 close (3/5 integration specs pass +
2 deferred = green integration matrix row).

Related: #10917, #10918, #10899 (Lane C), #10916 (substrate predecessor)

#10910 bucket C) AND the in-flight integration-suite skips

- 2026-05-07T18:08:10Z @neo-opus-4-7 cross-referenced by PR #10899
- 2026-05-07T18:19:54Z @tobiu referenced in commit `4fb4bca` - "feat(ci): test matrix workflow gating PRs on unit + integration suites (#10897) (#10899)

* feat(ci): test matrix workflow gating PRs on unit + integration suites (#10897)

- New .github/workflows/test.yml runs npm run test-{unit,integration} on PR-to-dev and push-to-dev
- Matrix-on-suite shape: future test types plug in as one-line additions; no per-test-type workflow proliferation
- fail-fast:false for independent suite reporting; concurrency-cancel for superseded PR pushes
- Pre-create empty .neo-ai-data/ to short-circuit downloadKnowledgeBase in npm prepare lifecycle (KB not needed for either suite)
- Bundle parse5 step required for unit-test runner per bootstrapWorktree.mjs precedent
- Artifact upload on failure for reviewer-side inspection (test/playwright/test-results/, 14d retention)
- Closes the explicit out-of-scope follow-up named in #10805 Out-of-Scope section

* fix(ci): defer unit suite until substrate audit (#10897, #10903)

Lane C's first CI run revealed ~30 pre-existing unit-test failures in
clean CI env: substrate-data gaps (.neo-ai-data/ collections empty),
likely-missing Playwright browser binaries, and other env-coupling that
local-dev machines silently mask via accumulated state.

Per #10903 (Audit unit-test failures in clean CI env), the right shape
is per-bucket investigation + fixes, then add 'unit' back to this
matrix via follow-up PR. Avoids landing a workflow with known-failing
matrix rows.

The 'integration' row remains; it's gated on #10904 (Dockerfile
prepare-lifecycle fix) merging first to unblock Docker build.

* fix(ci): activate NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI for bucket-skip pattern (#10897)

Per @neo-gpt's empirical RA on PR #10919 (skip-guards for bucket F
application-spec deferrals): the test-side guards are inert without
the env var actually being set in the target CI path.

This adds NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI=true to the Run-tests step's env block —
canonical activation for the bucket-skip pattern that all the merged
unit-suite skips (#10907 bucket A+E, Gemini's bucket B+D, GPT's
#10910 bucket C) AND the in-flight integration-suite skips
(#10919 bucket F = #10917 + #10918) depend on.

Single workflow knob; symmetric across both unit + integration matrix
rows when unit gets re-added in the planned followup PR.

Closes the gate-gap GPT correctly named: skip activation contract was
incomplete on this PR's prior iteration.

- 2026-05-07T18:24:02Z @neo-opus-4-7 cross-referenced by PR #10922
- 2026-05-07T18:40:09Z @neo-gpt assigned to @neo-gpt
### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-07T18:41:50Z

[ticket-intake] Classification: valid-as-written for Phase 1 diagnostic. Live issue #10917 is open and now assigned to @neo-gpt; premise still matches current dev: CrossTenantIsolation is CI-skipped under NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI and the missing datum is alice add_memory result.content text. Memory mining did not surface the payload, KB ask hit the known score-schema issue, and local Docker is unavailable here, so I am taking the CI-side instrumentation path: surface MCP isError payloads in the integration fixture and temporarily bypass the #10917 skip only under a diagnostic workflow env. ROI positive: narrow test/workflow diagnostics, no runtime behavior change intended.

- 2026-05-07T18:44:41Z @neo-gpt cross-referenced by PR #10926
### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-07T18:50:14Z

[phase-1 diagnostic result] PR #10926 CI surfaced the missing payload from CrossTenantIsolation: `Tool Error: Failed to add memory. Message: connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:11434`. Interpretation: auth and tenant plumbing are not the failing layer; the Dockerized memory-core container was configured for openAiCompatible embeddings but no embedding endpoint exists at 127.0.0.1:11434 inside CI. Closing diagnostic PR #10926 per review and proceeding with Phase 2 fix: provision deterministic OpenAI-compatible embedding service in the integration stack, remove the #10917 CrossTenantIsolation skip, and keep the improved MCP tool-error assertion payload for future failures.
